articles.headline: India Unveils Phase II of National Green Hydrogen Mission, Targets Enhanced Production Capacity
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved Phase II of the National Green Hydrogen Mission, focusing on scaling up production and demand creation.
• This phase aims to establish India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, leveraging its renewable energy potential.
• Key initiatives include further incentives for manufacturing electrolysers and developing green hydrogen production facilities.
• The mission seeks to reduce India's reliance on fossil fuel imports and contribute significantly to its net-zero emission targets by 2070.
• Emphasis is also placed on research and development, skill development, and pilot projects in hard-to-abate sectors like steel and shipping.

articles.headline: Bengaluru-Kamakhya Express Derails in Odisha, One Dead and Eight Injured
articles.explainer:
• Eleven cars of the Bengaluru-Kamakhya Superfast Express derailed near Nirgundi Train Station in Cuttack district, Odisha.
• The tragic incident resulted in one fatality and eight passengers sustaining injuries, prompting immediate rescue operations.
• Railway authorities have initiated a high-level inquiry to ascertain the exact cause of the derailment, focusing on track conditions, mechanical failure, or external factors.
• Emergency services, including NDRF and local police, were swiftly deployed to the site for rescue and relief efforts, with injured passengers shifted to nearby hospitals.
• The accident has led to temporary disruptions in train services on the affected route, with several trains diverted or cancelled.

Infrastructure: Railways

articles.imageDescription: Rescue workers assisting passengers near derailed train coaches.
articles.trivia: Indian Railways is the fourth largest railway network in the world by size, transporting millions of passengers daily.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Reinforces Right to Digital Privacy in Landmark Ruling on Data Collection
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court delivered a significant verdict, affirming that blanket data collection by private entities without explicit consent violates the fundamental Right to Digital Privacy.
• The ruling mandates stricter regulations for data fiduciaries, requiring transparent data handling practices and robust consent mechanisms.
• This judgment comes as a crucial interpretation of the Puttaswamy judgment (2017) in the context of evolving digital landscape and personal data protection.
• It is expected to significantly impact the upcoming Digital India Act and the existing Personal Data Protection Bill, urging amendments for stronger safeguards.
• The court emphasized the need to balance innovation and economic growth with the fundamental rights of individuals in the digital age.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
E-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential; citizens charters, transparency & accountability and institutional and other measures.
prelimsPoints: Puttaswamy judgment (2017) declared right to privacy as a fundamental right.
Current ruling focuses on digital privacy and data collection by private entities.
Mandates explicit consent for data collection.
Impacts Digital India Act and Personal Data Protection Bill.
Balance between innovation and fundamental rights.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of the Supreme Court's ruling on the 'Right to Digital Privacy' for individuals and businesses in India.
Discuss how this judgment will influence the legislative framework for data protection, particularly the Digital India Act.
Critically evaluate the challenges in enforcing digital privacy rights in a rapidly evolving technological landscape.
Examine the balance between national security, economic growth, and individual privacy in the context of digital governance.
articles.imageDescription: A gavel resting on a stack of law books with a digital interface overlay, symbolizing judicial intervention in digital matters.
articles.trivia: Article 21 of the Indian Constitution guarantees the 'Protection of Life and Personal Liberty', which the Supreme Court has interpreted to include the right to privacy.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Advanced Ocean Observation Satellite 'OceanSat-4'
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched OceanSat-4, an advanced Earth observation satellite dedicated to oceanographic studies.
• Carried aboard the PSLV-C58 rocket from Satish Dhawan Space Centre, Sriharikota, the satellite will provide critical data for marine weather forecasting, fisheries, and climate research.
• OceanSat-4 features improved sensors for sea surface temperature, ocean colour, and wind vector measurements, enhancing India's capability in ocean monitoring.
• This mission reinforces India's commitment to space-based applications for societal benefit and contributes to global climate change studies.
• The data from OceanSat-4 will be instrumental for disaster warning systems, especially for cyclones and tsunamis in the Indian Ocean region.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- Developments and their Applications and Effects in Everyday Life
Achievements of Indians in Science & Technology; Indigenization of Technology and Developing New Technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space

articles.imageDescription: A PSLV rocket launching into space, with 'OceanSat-4' text overlay.
articles.trivia: ISRO's first dedicated ocean observation satellite, OceanSat-1, was launched in 1999.

articles.headline: India-France Strategic Dialogue Concludes, Emphasizes Defence & Green Energy Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-France Strategic Dialogue concluded in New Delhi, focusing on enhancing bilateral cooperation in defence, security, and emerging technologies.
• Both nations reiterated their commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific, agreeing on joint efforts to address regional security challenges.
• A significant outcome was the renewed push for collaboration in green energy technologies, including nuclear energy and hydrogen, aligning with global climate goals.
• Discussions also covered counter-terrorism, cyber security, and strengthening multilateralism through shared values and interests.
• The dialogue underscored the robust strategic partnership between India and France, vital for stability in a complex global environment.

articles.headline: RBI's Latest Report Indicates Moderating Inflation for Q1 2025-26, Cautions on Food Prices
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) released its latest Monetary Policy Report, projecting a moderating trend for headline inflation in the first quarter of fiscal year 2025-26.
• The report noted a easing of core inflation, driven by stable demand and supply-side measures, offering some respite to consumers.
• However, the RBI cautioned about the persistent upside risks from volatile food prices, particularly due to unseasonal weather patterns impacting agricultural output.
• The central bank reiterated its commitment to achieving the medium-term inflation target of 4%, while supporting economic growth.
• This outlook will be crucial for the upcoming Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) meeting, influencing decisions on interest rates.

articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) consists of six members – three officials from the RBI and three external members nominated by the Government of India.

articles.headline: Union Ministry Launches 'Smart Cities 2.0' with Focus on Climate-Resilient Urban Development
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs officially launched 'Smart Cities 2.0', building on the successes and lessons learned from the first phase.
• This new phase places a strong emphasis on climate-resilient infrastructure, green mobility, and citizen-centric governance in urban areas.
• It aims to integrate advanced technologies like AI and IoT for efficient urban planning, waste management, and public safety.
• The mission seeks to create more sustainable and livable cities by promoting circular economy principles and public-private partnerships.
• Funding mechanisms and selection criteria for new cities under this phase are expected to prioritize environmental sustainability and digital transformation.

articles.headline: Government Unveils New Policy to Boost Domestic Medical Device Manufacturing
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare, in collaboration with the Department of Pharmaceuticals, launched a comprehensive new policy to catalyze domestic medical device manufacturing.
• The policy aims to reduce India's import dependence, enhance self-reliance (Atmanirbhar Bharat), and position India as a global manufacturing hub for medical devices.
• Key provisions include production-linked incentive (PLI) schemes, establishment of medical device parks, and streamlined regulatory approvals.
• Emphasis is placed on research and development, skill development, and promoting indigenous innovation in high-tech medical equipment.
• This initiative is expected to improve healthcare accessibility, affordability, and quality by ensuring a steady supply of advanced medical devices.

articles.headline: India and Bangladesh Inaugurate Three New Connectivity Projects, Boost Regional Trade
articles.explainer:
• India and Bangladesh jointly inaugurated three new connectivity projects, including a railway line, a port facility, and a cross-border power transmission line.
• These projects aim to enhance trade, transit, and people-to-people connectivity between the two nations, strengthening bilateral ties.
• The railway line is expected to facilitate cargo movement, reducing logistical costs and time for businesses in both countries.
• The new port facility will improve maritime trade routes, offering an alternative access point for goods to India's northeastern states.
• The power transmission line underscores cooperation in energy security, promoting regional energy grid integration.

articles.headline: Union Environment Ministry Notifies New Tiger Reserve in Rajasthan, Boosts Conservation Efforts
articles.explainer:
• The Union Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change officially notified a new Tiger Reserve in Rajasthan, marking a significant step for wildlife conservation.
• This new reserve aims to provide an additional protected habitat for the endangered Bengal Tiger, enhancing corridor connectivity between existing reserves.
• The declaration comes after extensive ecological surveys and consultations with local communities, ensuring sustainable management practices.
• It is expected to bolster tiger populations, protect biodiversity, and promote eco-tourism in the region, contributing to local livelihoods.
• India currently hosts over 70% of the world's tiger population, and new reserves are crucial for sustaining this growth.

articles.headline: India Unveils Enhanced National Cyber Security Strategy to Combat Evolving Threats
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) released an updated National Cyber Security Strategy 2025, addressing the rapidly evolving cyber threat landscape.
• The strategy focuses on bolstering critical information infrastructure protection, promoting cyber hygiene, and fostering international cooperation.
• Key pillars include developing a skilled cybersecurity workforce, encouraging indigenous technologies, and establishing a robust incident response mechanism.
• It aims to create a secure and resilient cyberspace for citizens, businesses, and government, safeguarding digital assets and privacy.
• The strategy also emphasizes public-private partnerships and awareness campaigns to build a collective defense against cyberattacks.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Comprehensive Electoral Reforms, Including Online Voting Pilot
articles.explainer:
• A Joint Parliamentary Committee submitted its report on electoral reforms, proposing a range of measures to enhance transparency and accessibility in elections.
• Key recommendations include a pilot project for online voting for specific sections of the electorate, such as NRIs and service personnel, leveraging secure blockchain technology.
• The panel also suggested strengthening the Election Commission's powers, campaign finance reforms, and measures to curb misinformation during elections.
• It emphasized the need for a unified electoral roll and greater use of digital platforms for voter registration and awareness.
• The report is expected to trigger debates on the future of electoral processes in India and potential legislative changes.

articles.headline: Ministry of Education Reviews NEP 2020 Implementation Progress, Focus on Foundational Learning
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education conducted a comprehensive review of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 implementation, highlighting key achievements and challenges.
• The review emphasized the progress made in foundational literacy and numeracy (FLN) programs, crucial for early childhood education.
• Discussions also focused on integrating vocational education, digital learning initiatives, and teacher training programs across states.
• Challenges identified include infrastructure gaps, digital divide in remote areas, and the need for greater state-level coordination.
• The Ministry reiterated its commitment to achieving the NEP's transformative goals by 2030, with continuous monitoring and adaptive strategies.

articles.headline: Government Boosts Policy Support for Climate-Resilient Agriculture and Crop Diversification
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare announced enhanced policy support and financial incentives for climate-resilient agriculture practices.
• The initiative aims to help farmers adapt to changing weather patterns, reduce crop losses, and ensure food security amidst climate change challenges.
• Key measures include promoting drought-resistant crop varieties, precision irrigation techniques, and organic farming methods.
• Emphasis is also placed on crop diversification away from water-intensive crops, encouraging cultivation of millets and pulses.
• The policy integrates scientific research, farmer training, and access to climate-smart technologies to build a sustainable agricultural sector.

articles.headline: ASI Initiates Major Restoration Project for 12th Century Temple in Karnataka

• The temple, known for its intricate carvings and unique architectural style, is a crucial example of Hoysala art and a UNESCO World Heritage site nominee.
• The project involves scientific conservation techniques, structural stabilization, and detailed documentation to preserve its historical and cultural integrity.
• This initiative is part of a broader effort by ASI to protect and promote India's rich architectural heritage and attract cultural tourism.
• Local communities and heritage enthusiasts are actively involved, highlighting the importance of public participation in conservation efforts.

articles.headline: India Advocates for Equitable Climate Finance at Global Summit in Paris

• India reiterated its stance on 'Common but Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities' (CBDR-RC) in climate action and financing.
• Discussions centered on mobilizing financial resources for adaptation and mitigation, technology transfer, and capacity building in developing countries.
• India highlighted its own initiatives in renewable energy and climate resilience, emphasizing the need for global solidarity.
• The summit aimed to build consensus on a new collective quantified goal (NCQG) for climate finance post-2025.

articles.headline: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission Achieves Milestone with 500 Million ABHA IDs Generated

articles.headline: India Participates in Global Chip Manufacturing Summit, Pitches for Investment
articles.explainer:
• A high-level Indian delegation participated in the Global Chip Manufacturing Summit, aiming to attract investment and foster collaborations in semiconductor production.
• India highlighted its 'Semicon India' program and production-linked incentive (PLI) schemes designed to establish a robust semiconductor ecosystem.
• Discussions focused on supply chain resilience, R&D partnerships, and talent development in the critical sector of chip manufacturing.
• The government reiterated its commitment to making India a significant player in the global semiconductor value chain, crucial for digital economy and strategic autonomy.
• The summit provided a platform for dialogues with leading global semiconductor firms and technology providers.

articles.headline: Border Roads Organisation Completes Strategic Road Network in Eastern Sector
articles.explainer:
• The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) announced the completion of a crucial strategic road network in India's eastern sector, enhancing connectivity to remote border areas.
• This infrastructure development is vital for improving troop mobility, logistics supply, and overall border security along the challenging terrain.
• The project involved constructing high-altitude roads, bridges, and tunnels, overcoming significant geographical and climatic obstacles.
• It is expected to boost economic development in the border regions by facilitating trade and tourism, and integrating remote communities.
• The BRO's continuous efforts are key to strengthening India's defense preparedness and maintaining territorial integrity.

articles.headline: India-Pakistan Permanent Indus Commission Holds Annual Meeting, Discusses Water Issues
articles.explainer:
• The Permanent Indus Commission, comprising water commissioners from India and Pakistan, held its annual meeting to discuss various aspects of the Indus Waters Treaty.
• The dialogue focused on sharing hydrological data, resolving technical issues related to ongoing projects, and ensuring adherence to the treaty provisions.
• India reiterated its commitment to the spirit of the treaty while emphasizing its right to utilize its share of water resources for domestic and agricultural purposes.
• The meeting serves as a crucial platform for bilateral cooperation and conflict resolution on water-related matters between the two nuclear-armed neighbors.
• Despite broader political tensions, the Indus Waters Treaty has largely endured as a framework for water sharing.

articles.headline: GST Collections for March 2025 Show Robust Growth, Indicate Economic Momentum
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Finance announced Goods and Services Tax (GST) collections for March 2025, showing robust year-on-year growth, indicating sustained economic momentum.
• The strong collections are attributed to increased economic activity, better compliance, and effective anti-evasion measures by tax authorities.
• This consistent growth in GST revenue provides fiscal space for the government to invest in infrastructure and welfare schemes.
• The positive trend reflects the formalization of the economy and the success of the GST regime in streamlining indirect taxation.
• Experts predict continued growth, supporting India's economic recovery trajectory.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the National Green Hydrogen Mission, consider the following statements:
1. The mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export.
2. Production-Linked Incentive (PLI) schemes are a key component to incentivize electrolyser manufacturing.
3. Hard-to-abate sectors like steel and shipping are not targeted in the initial phases of the mission.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The mission explicitly aims to position India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export. Statement 2 is correct: PLI schemes for manufacturing electrolysers are a key incentive. Statement 3 is incorrect: Hard-to-abate sectors like steel, shipping, and heavy transport are specifically targeted for green hydrogen adoption.

prelimsMocks.question: The recent Supreme Court ruling reinforcing the 'Right to Digital Privacy' is a significant interpretation in the context of which landmark judgment?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Supreme Court's recent ruling on digital privacy is a crucial interpretation of the K.S. Puttaswamy judgment (2017), which declared the right to privacy as a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Indian Constitution. The other cases relate to the basic structure doctrine and federalism.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following is the primary objective of ISRO's newly launched 'OceanSat-4' satellite?
options: (c) Oceanographic studies and marine weather forecasting
(d) Providing high-speed internet connectivity across rural areas
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) Oceanographic studies and marine weather forecasting
prelimsMocks.explanation: OceanSat-4 is an advanced Earth observation satellite specifically dedicated to oceanographic studies. Its primary objectives include providing critical data for marine weather forecasting, fisheries, and climate research, with improved sensors for sea surface temperature, ocean colour, and wind vector measurements.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the 'Smart Cities 2.0' mission:
1. It focuses on climate-resilient infrastructure and green mobility.
2. It aims to integrate technologies like AI and IoT for urban planning.
3. The mission is primarily funded by international aid organizations.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statements 1 and 2 are correct. Smart Cities 2.0 emphasizes climate-resilient infrastructure, green mobility, and the integration of AI and IoT for efficient urban planning. Statement 3 is incorrect; the mission is primarily funded by the central and state governments, often leveraging public-private partnerships, not primarily international aid.

prelimsMocks.question: The Permanent Indus Commission, which recently held its annual meeting, is responsible for implementing which bilateral agreement between India and Pakistan?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Permanent Indus Commission is a bilateral commission of officials from India and Pakistan, established under the Indus Waters Treaty, which was signed in 1960. Its mandate is to implement and manage the provisions of the treaty concerning the sharing of the Indus River System waters.

mainsMocks.question: Analyze the strategic importance of India's National Green Hydrogen Mission in achieving its energy security goals and climate commitments. Discuss the key challenges and opportunities in scaling up green hydrogen production in the country.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Infrastructure: Energy; Environmental Pollution & Degradation; Economic Development
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the National Green Hydrogen Mission and its objectives. Discuss its strategic importance for energy independence (reducing fossil fuel imports), climate commitments (net-zero by 2070), and economic growth (new industry, jobs, exports). Detail challenges like high production costs, need for massive renewable energy infrastructure, water availability, and logistics. Highlight opportunities such as India's renewable energy potential, PLI schemes, R&D in electrolysers, and demand creation in hard-to-abate sectors. Conclude with India's potential to be a global leader.

mainsMocks.question: The recent Supreme Court ruling on the 'Right to Digital Privacy' marks a crucial evolution in India's legal framework. Discuss the implications of this judgment for individual rights and the regulatory environment for data fiduciaries. How might it influence the upcoming Digital India Act?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Indian Constitution—fundamental rights; Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors; E-governance
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by contextualizing the ruling with the Puttaswamy judgment. Discuss implications for individuals (enhanced control over personal data, protection against arbitrary collection) and for data fiduciaries (mandate for explicit consent, greater transparency, stricter compliance). Analyze how it will shape the Digital India Act, potentially leading to stronger privacy safeguards, clearer definitions of consent, and robust enforcement mechanisms. Discuss balancing innovation with privacy and the challenges of implementation.

mainsMocks.question: Evaluate the success of India's 'Smart Cities Mission' since its inception and discuss how the newly launched 'Smart Cities 2.0' addresses the evolving challenges of urban development, particularly concerning climate resilience and sustainable mobility.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors; Urbanization, their problems and their remedies; GS3: Infrastructure
mainsMocks.approach: Briefly introduce the Smart Cities Mission's objectives and initial achievements/shortcomings. Explain how Smart Cities 2.0 incorporates lessons learned, focusing on new priorities like climate resilience (green buildings, disaster management, renewable energy), sustainable mobility (EVs, public transport, NMT), and citizen-centric governance. Discuss the challenges of urban development in India (population growth, infrastructure deficit, pollution) and how the new phase aims to tackle these through integrated planning, technology (AI, IoT), and public-private partnerships. Conclude on the vision for more livable and sustainable Indian cities.

GS1.headline: ASI Initiates Major Restoration Project for 12th Century Temple in Karnataka
GS1.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) has launched a significant restoration and conservation project for a dilapidated 12th-century Hoysala temple in Karnataka.
• The temple, known for its intricate carvings and unique architectural style, is a crucial example of Hoysala art and a UNESCO World Heritage site nominee.
• The project involves scientific conservation techniques, structural stabilization, and detailed documentation to preserve its historical and cultural integrity.
• This initiative is part of a broader effort by ASI to protect and promote India's rich architectural heritage and attract cultural tourism.
• Local communities and heritage enthusiasts are actively involved, highlighting the importance of public participation in conservation efforts.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Reinforces Right to Digital Privacy in Landmark Ruling on Data Collection
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court delivered a significant verdict, affirming that blanket data collection by private entities without explicit consent violates the fundamental Right to Digital Privacy.
• The ruling mandates stricter regulations for data fiduciaries, requiring transparent data handling practices and robust consent mechanisms.
• This judgment comes as a crucial interpretation of the Puttaswamy judgment (2017) in the context of evolving digital landscape and personal data protection.
• It is expected to significantly impact the upcoming Digital India Act and the existing Personal Data Protection Bill, urging amendments for stronger safeguards.
• The court emphasized the need to balance innovation and economic growth with the fundamental rights of individuals in the digital age.

GS2.explainer:

• Both nations reiterated their commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific, agreeing on joint efforts to address regional security challenges.
• A significant outcome was the renewed push for collaboration in green energy technologies, including nuclear energy and hydrogen, aligning with global climate goals.
• Discussions also covered counter-terrorism, cyber security, and strengthening multilateralism through shared values and interests.
• The dialogue underscored the robust strategic partnership between India and France, vital for stability in a complex global environment.

GS2.headline: India and Bangladesh Inaugurate Three New Connectivity Projects, Boost Regional Trade
GS2.explainer:
• India and Bangladesh jointly inaugurated three new connectivity projects, including a railway line, a port facility, and a cross-border power transmission line.
• These projects aim to enhance trade, transit, and people-to-people connectivity between the two nations, strengthening bilateral ties.
• The railway line is expected to facilitate cargo movement, reducing logistical costs and time for businesses in both countries.
• The new port facility will improve maritime trade routes, offering an alternative access point for goods to India's northeastern states.
• The power transmission line underscores cooperation in energy security, promoting regional energy grid integration.

GS2.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Comprehensive Electoral Reforms, Including Online Voting Pilot
GS2.explainer:
• A Joint Parliamentary Committee submitted its report on electoral reforms, proposing a range of measures to enhance transparency and accessibility in elections.
• Key recommendations include a pilot project for online voting for specific sections of the electorate, such as NRIs and service personnel, leveraging secure blockchain technology.
• The panel also suggested strengthening the Election Commission's powers, campaign finance reforms, and measures to curb misinformation during elections.
• It emphasized the need for a unified electoral roll and greater use of digital platforms for voter registration and awareness.
• The report is expected to trigger debates on the future of electoral processes in India and potential legislative changes.

GS3.headline: Bengaluru-Kamakhya Express Derails in Odisha, One Dead and Eight Injured
GS3.explainer:
• Eleven cars of the Bengaluru-Kamakhya Superfast Express derailed near Nirgundi Train Station in Cuttack district, Odisha.
• The tragic incident resulted in one fatality and eight passengers sustaining injuries, prompting immediate rescue operations.
• Railway authorities have initiated a high-level inquiry to ascertain the exact cause of the derailment, focusing on track conditions, mechanical failure, or external factors.
• Emergency services, including NDRF and local police, were swiftly deployed to the site for rescue and relief efforts, with injured passengers shifted to nearby hospitals.
• The accident has led to temporary disruptions in train services on the affected route, with several trains diverted or cancelled.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Advanced Ocean Observation Satellite 'OceanSat-4'
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched OceanSat-4, an advanced Earth observation satellite dedicated to oceanographic studies.
• Carried aboard the PSLV-C58 rocket from Satish Dhawan Space Centre, Sriharikota, the satellite will provide critical data for marine weather forecasting, fisheries, and climate research.
• OceanSat-4 features improved sensors for sea surface temperature, ocean colour, and wind vector measurements, enhancing India's capability in ocean monitoring.
• This mission reinforces India's commitment to space-based applications for societal benefit and contributes to global climate change studies.
• The data from OceanSat-4 will be instrumental for disaster warning systems, especially for cyclones and tsunamis in the Indian Ocean region.

GS3.headline: RBI's Latest Report Indicates Moderating Inflation for Q1 2025-26, Cautions on Food Prices
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) released its latest Monetary Policy Report, projecting a moderating trend for headline inflation in the first quarter of fiscal year 2025-26.
• The report noted a easing of core inflation, driven by stable demand and supply-side measures, offering some respite to consumers.
• However, the RBI cautioned about the persistent upside risks from volatile food prices, particularly due to unseasonal weather patterns impacting agricultural output.
• The central bank reiterated its commitment to achieving the medium-term inflation target of 4%, while supporting economic growth.
• This outlook will be crucial for the upcoming Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) meeting, influencing decisions on interest rates.

GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) consists of six members – three officials from the RBI and three external members nominated by the Government of India.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils New Policy to Boost Domestic Medical Device Manufacturing
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare, in collaboration with the Department of Pharmaceuticals, launched a comprehensive new policy to catalyze domestic medical device manufacturing.
• The policy aims to reduce India's import dependence, enhance self-reliance (Atmanirbhar Bharat), and position India as a global manufacturing hub for medical devices.
• Key provisions include production-linked incentive (PLI) schemes, establishment of medical device parks, and streamlined regulatory approvals.
• Emphasis is placed on research and development, skill development, and promoting indigenous innovation in high-tech medical equipment.
• This initiative is expected to improve healthcare accessibility, affordability, and quality by ensuring a steady supply of advanced medical devices.

GS3.headline: Union Environment Ministry Notifies New Tiger Reserve in Rajasthan, Boosts Conservation Efforts
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change officially notified a new Tiger Reserve in Rajasthan, marking a significant step for wildlife conservation.
• This new reserve aims to provide an additional protected habitat for the endangered Bengal Tiger, enhancing corridor connectivity between existing reserves.
• The declaration comes after extensive ecological surveys and consultations with local communities, ensuring sustainable management practices.
• It is expected to bolster tiger populations, protect biodiversity, and promote eco-tourism in the region, contributing to local livelihoods.
• India currently hosts over 70% of the world's tiger population, and new reserves are crucial for sustaining this growth.

GS3.headline: India Unveils Enhanced National Cyber Security Strategy to Combat Evolving Threats
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) released an updated National Cyber Security Strategy 2025, addressing the rapidly evolving cyber threat landscape.
• The strategy focuses on bolstering critical information infrastructure protection, promoting cyber hygiene, and fostering international cooperation.
• Key pillars include developing a skilled cybersecurity workforce, encouraging indigenous technologies, and establishing a robust incident response mechanism.
• It aims to create a secure and resilient cyberspace for citizens, businesses, and government, safeguarding digital assets and privacy.
• The strategy also emphasizes public-private partnerships and awareness campaigns to build a collective defense against cyberattacks.

GS3.headline: Government Boosts Policy Support for Climate-Resilient Agriculture and Crop Diversification
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare announced enhanced policy support and financial incentives for climate-resilient agriculture practices.
• The initiative aims to help farmers adapt to changing weather patterns, reduce crop losses, and ensure food security amidst climate change challenges.
• Key measures include promoting drought-resistant crop varieties, precision irrigation techniques, and organic farming methods.
• Emphasis is also placed on crop diversification away from water-intensive crops, encouraging cultivation of millets and pulses.
• The policy integrates scientific research, farmer training, and access to climate-smart technologies to build a sustainable agricultural sector.

GS3.headline: NITI Aayog Proposes National Framework for Sustainable Urban Transport and Green Mobility
GS3.explainer:
• NITI Aayog released a discussion paper outlining a comprehensive national framework for promoting sustainable urban transport and green mobility solutions.
• The framework advocates for integrated public transport systems, electric vehicle (EV) adoption, and pedestrian/cyclist-friendly infrastructure.
• It emphasizes the need for innovative financing mechanisms and public-private partnerships to scale up green mobility projects.
• The policy aims to reduce urban congestion, improve air quality, and lower carbon emissions in major Indian cities.
• Recommendations include incentivizing state governments for adopting green transport policies and developing smart mobility solutions.

GS3.headline: India Advocates for Equitable Climate Finance at Global Summit in Paris
GS3.explainer:
• India's delegation actively participated in the Global Climate Finance Summit in Paris, advocating for increased and equitable climate finance from developed nations.
• India reiterated its stance on 'Common but Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities' (CBDR-RC) in climate action and financing.
• Discussions centered on mobilizing financial resources for adaptation and mitigation, technology transfer, and capacity building in developing countries.
• India highlighted its own initiatives in renewable energy and climate resilience, emphasizing the need for global solidarity.
• The summit aimed to build consensus on a new collective quantified goal (NCQG) for climate finance post-2025.

GS3.headline: Border Roads Organisation Completes Strategic Road Network in Eastern Sector
GS3.explainer:
• The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) announced the completion of a crucial strategic road network in India's eastern sector, enhancing connectivity to remote border areas.
• This infrastructure development is vital for improving troop mobility, logistics supply, and overall border security along the challenging terrain.
• The project involved constructing high-altitude roads, bridges, and tunnels, overcoming significant geographical and climatic obstacles.
• It is expected to boost economic development in the border regions by facilitating trade and tourism, and integrating remote communities.
• The BRO's continuous efforts are key to strengthening India's defense preparedness and maintaining territorial integrity.

GS3.headline: GST Collections for March 2025 Show Robust Growth, Indicate Economic Momentum
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Finance announced Goods and Services Tax (GST) collections for March 2025, showing robust year-on-year growth, indicating sustained economic momentum.
• The strong collections are attributed to increased economic activity, better compliance, and effective anti-evasion measures by tax authorities.
• This consistent growth in GST revenue provides fiscal space for the government to invest in infrastructure and welfare schemes.
• The positive trend reflects the formalization of the economy and the success of the GST regime in streamlining indirect taxation.
• Experts predict continued growth, supporting India's economic recovery trajectory.
